theory.canonical_statement: ID 429 defines the protocol for extracting data from the Atemporal Channel, where temporal ordering collapses onto null surfaces. While the Energy Channel focuses on flux, this protocol utilizes the geometric Berry phase gamman acquired by the quantum state as it evolves through the lemniscatic parameter space. Because this phase is gauge invariant and topologically protected, it remains robust against the local thermal perturbations of the torus environment. Readout is achieved through single shot quantum state tomography, which reconstructs the density matrix of the extracted information states. By mapping the Berry curvature Fnu, the system translates atemporal quantum correlations into classical observational data within the Temporal Channel. This process is governed by the Holevo bound, ensuring the channel capacity respects the energy constraints of the available vacuum potential.
